commands.modern_energy.energy.consume.fa: %sConsume failed: could not precisely consume %s from %s; now %s
commands.modern_energy.energy.help:
Modern Energy debug commands:
/modernenergy energy help - Show this help message.
/modernenergy energy info [id] - Show all or one debug energy buffer.
/modernenergy energy create <id> <capacity> [energy] [maxInsert] [maxExtract] - Create or replace a buffer; omitted rates default to capacity.
/modernenergy energy insert <id> <amount> [simulate] - Insert ME into a buffer.
/modernenergy energy extract <id> <amount> [simulate] - Extract ME from a buffer.
/modernenergy energy consume <id> <amount> [simulate] - Precisely consume ME; fails if energy is insufficient or above max output.
/modernenergy energy set-rate <id> <maxInsert> <maxExtract> - Set max input/output.
/modernenergy energy set-electrical <id> <voltage> <resistance> [applyRate] - Set voltage/resistance; current and ME/t are derived from them.
/modernenergy energy transfer <source> <target> <amount> [simulate] - Transfer ME between two buffers.
/modernenergy energy clear [id] - Clear one buffer, or clear all buffers.
Note: when simulate is true, the command previews the result without changing state. ME means Mechatronic Energy. Electrical rate uses P = V * I and I = V / R. Default electrical profile uses China mains reference: 220 V, 50 Hz, 10 A reference current.
commands.modern_energy.energy.none: No debug energy buffers exist. Use /modernenergy energy help for usage.
commands.modern_energy.energy.same_buffe: Source and target must be different.
commands.modern_energy.energy.set_electr: Applied derived ME/t to max input/output.
